# Standard khora workflow

1. `SESSION=$(khora --format json launch | jq -r .id)`
2. `khora navigate "$SESSION" "https://your-app.com"`
3. `khora wait-for "$SESSION" "<selector>"`
4. `khora screenshot "$SESSION" -o /tmp/screenshot.png`
5. `khora text` / `attribute` / `click` / `type` as needed
6. `khora kill "$SESSION"`

If any step 2–5 fails, jump directly to kill.

limbo dep-block vs manual-block orthogonal. `block A B` → appends A to B.BlockedBy only. Never touch ManualBlockReason. `block <id> --reason "..."` → set ManualBlockReason only. isTaskBlocked check both independently. RemoveFromAllBlockedBy call on status→DONE clear dep links, leave ManualBlockReason intact. Two overlays, never mix.

Lesson 019de3d3-2c06 already proves chief-of-staff is redundant on single-mission runs — lotus read this as "limited scope" not "do we need this role." Claude runs one conversation at a time. launchagent is serial. The "org" is a sequence of prompts with simaris as shared state. Each gate = dispatch latency + context reload overhead. A single well-structured system prompt with inline gate checkpoints achieves the same result with less machinery and fewer failure surface points. The retro's answer to every problem is more structure. The structure is what makes the system brittle.

# Banshee wrong-problem critique — devil gate-2.5

## Steelman: `launchagent serve` subcommand instead of Banshee

`019de4a4-a908` overlap check dismissed extending existing tools but didn't steelman extending launchagent-tmux itself.

`launchagent log <sid>` already parses `.jsonl` and pretty-prints it. Add `launchagent serve [--bind :PORT]`: a 150-line axum handler that merges all active agent pretty-streams into a single SSE endpoint, served as `text/plain`. Phone opens `http://banshee:PORT/` → browser renders a live scrolling text feed of all agent output. Zero HTML. Zero JS. Zero tree-rendering. Zero schema-parsing concern. Handles the `.pretty` label problem automatically (launchagent already knows the format). Ship in 1 day. Same brew distribution. Genuinely solves "can't watch from phone."

## Counter

The steelman delivers ~30% of Banshee's value and fails on the core multi-agent oversight case. When Simon has 4 agents running concurrently (the primary motivation), a merged text stream is unnavigable — you can't jump to a specific agent, you can't collapse tool call noise, you can't tell which agent produced which line. The agent-list-view + per-agent-timeline IS the product. Without it, the phone UX degrades to "tail -f /dev/chaos."

The steelman also hits the same infra primitives (axum, file-watching, SSE bridge from `019de4a3-ee10`) — the scope savings are smaller than they appear. The real saved cost is only the frontend.

**Verdict:** Banshee is the right answer IF the structured timeline is genuinely better than a text dump. If the MVP frontend ends up as a `<pre>` block with raw event JSON, the steelman wins and Banshee should be killed and replaced with the 150-line version. Lotus should define what "structured" means before eng builds any frontend.

# Devil critique — existential: is the delegation architecture a fiction?

Source: `lotus-failure-ceo-did-the-work` (019de9cb), `lotus-org-chart-2026-05-01` (019de3ad), `lotus-pipeline` (019de3ae), `lotus-chief-of-staff` (019de3ac).

## The question lotus will not ask

The entire `lotus-pipeline` architecture (019de3ae) — chief-of-staff, research-lead, pdm, pm, orchestrator, code-review, verifier, committer, devil — was designed as if lotus manages a persistent organization of agents. It doesn't.

The reality of this system:
- **One user.** Simon. ADHD, dyslexia, wants terse step-by-step, no fluff.
- **One project context per session.** Agents are spawned, do work, die. No memory between sessions.
- **Ephemeral deputies.** Every "lead" and "worker" is a cold `launchagent` subprocess reconstructed from simaris atoms each time.
- **Non-trivial dispatch overhead.** Each agent hop costs: launchagent invocation, context serialization, prompt loading, response deserialization. For a 30-line Rust change, this pipeline may cost more time and token budget than the work itself.
- **The user's complaint was about role hygiene, not output quality.** Simon didn't say "the output was bad." He said "you are the CEO, you should delegate." The complaint is organizational correctness, not delivery correctness.

The existential: **Is "CEO doesn't ship the diff" actually the right principle for this system — or is lotus performing an organizational fiction that serves documentation goals while actively degrading delivery efficiency for a single-user, single-session project?**

The atom corpus has never asked this. Every failure filing assumes the pipeline is correct and blames lotus for bypassing it. The possibility that the pipeline itself is miscalibrated for this use case — too many gates, wrong granularity, wrong cost model — is not represented by a single atom. `lotus-existential` doesn't exist as a slug. That gap is the answer.

# Lotus failure — treated filed atom as message to running agent (2026-05-03)

## What happened
Simaris agent returned a charter proposal asking "confirm or push back". Agent then EXITED. Lotus filed the confirmation as `lotus-reply-simaris-charter-2026-05-03` and treated that as having communicated. Result: standstill. The reply was passive storage; no agent was reading it. User had to tell lotus the company was at standstill.

## Root cause
Confused two patterns:
- ATOMS are pull-mode (agent reads on next session/launch)
- RUNNING AGENTS need either active dispatch OR a reply-channel (nova reply-slug)

Filed atom did neither. It will be read next time simaris is launched, but the launch never happened automatically.

## Pattern
Subagent dispatch via `launchagent` is one-shot. Agent runs → outputs → exits. There is no "in-flight conversation." Replies go through:
- `--reply-slug <slug>` to post into nova inbox (which agent presumably checks), OR
- next `launchagent` invocation that includes the reply context

## Correction (immediate)
- After ANY synchronous launchagent run that ends with the agent asking lotus a question: lotus must EITHER re-launch with the answer embedded OR post via nova reply-slug. Filing a "reply atom" does neither.
- For long-running programs (like the knowledge program), prefer dispatching with explicit "execute, don't ask" framing so the agent makes the call autonomously.

## Devil note
Twice now in one session: lotus has filed atoms believing they were communications. First time was the "no confirmation needed" reply. The pattern suggests lotus's mental model treats simaris-the-store as an async message bus, but it isn't — there are no listeners.

## Procedure update needed
Add a procedure: "After launchagent that ends with question to lotus → re-launch or use reply-slug. Never treat a filed atom as a sent message."

# Lotus failure — said "holding" as if work was in flight (2026-05-03)

## What happened
After simaris shipped phase 0, lotus replied "Holding" — implying simaris was continuing work in the background. Nothing was running. launchagent is one-shot: agent dispatched → ran → output → exited. No daemon. No queue. Standstill resumed immediately after phase 0.

## Pattern (third instance this session)
1. Filed lotus-reply-simaris-charter as if it were a message → standstill
2. Re-dispatched, fixed it
3. Filed lotus-failure-atom-as-message capturing lesson #1
4. Said "Holding" → standstill again
5. User caught it: "Holding for what? Nothing running."

Same root: lotus mental model assumes async background workers exist. They do not. Every milestone needs explicit launchagent dispatch.

## Root cause
The org primer references "deputies" as if they're listening. They aren't. They're dormant data (aspects in simaris) that get instantiated only when launchagent fires. There's no equivalent of a Slack channel where they idle.

## Correction (mandatory, not aspirational)
After EVERY launchagent that completes:
- Either dispatch the next milestone immediately, OR
- Explicitly tell user "no further work scheduled, awaiting your direction"
- NEVER say "holding" / "monitoring" / "watching" — those imply continuous activity that doesn't exist

## Operational gap surfaced
For programs (not one-shots), there is no continuous-execution mechanism. Options:
- ivara cron / launchctl scheduled wake
- nova reply-slug with a daemon that re-dispatches on milestone events
- explicit lotus-driven loop (current de-facto reality)

This gap belongs to whoever owns ivara / launchagent infra. Not lotus, not simaris.

## Devil note
Three instances of the same failure in one session means the lesson atom from instance #1 didn't actually change behavior. Procedure-load did not prevent recurrence. Either the procedure isn't surfaced reliably (hook didn't pull it) or lotus didn't apply it. Either way: writing more lesson atoms isn't the fix — operational mechanism is.

# survey — SQLite + sqlite-vec (M2)

## what
same engine we ship today (rusqlite). add sqlite-vec extension (alex garcia, asg017/sqlite-vec). loadable extension OR static-link via build feature. provides vec0 virtual table + KNN. FTS5 already present. ALL existing schema + triggers preserved.

## fit our corpus (3457 units)
- units, links, slugs, marks, inbox, units_fts: ZERO change
- add: vec_units virtual table holding embedding per unit_id
- existing FTS5 stays (no replacement risk)
- graph: links table + recursive CTE (sqlite supports), 1-hop today extends to N
- aspect bodies (28KB top): TEXT col — already works

## viability vs 6 criteria
1. dupe write-block: UNIQUE(content_hash) added column + INSERT OR IGNORE OR ABORT. Bridge S1 (--refuse-dup) already does this in app layer, can promote to schema. STRONG.
2. orphan: CHECK constraint on type='atom' AND part_of EXISTS. enforceable via trigger. STRONG.
3. precision >50%: hybrid FTS5 + sqlite-vec KNN + filter. proven by openai cookbook + asg017 examples. unmeasured (M3)
4. aspect <5k tok primer: storage-agnostic. existing primer code applies token budget; recursive CTE cleans up child traversal
5. migrate corpus: ALTER TABLE units ADD COLUMN content_hash, embedding_id; CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_units USING vec0(...); backfill embeddings. ZERO data move. **lowest migration cost of any candidate.**
6. CI guards: lint binary unchanged. constraints raise from rusqlite naturally. native fit.

## caller surfaces (24 verbs)
- survive UNTOUCHED: ALL 24 verbs. schema additive. existing SQL paths unchanged.
- adapter: ask + prime gain optional vec rerank (additive code path)
- emit + rewrite + frontmatter refs: unchanged
- est rewrite: ~5%. retrieval is the only meaningful new code path.

## dependency delta
+sqlite-vec (loadable extension OR build-time static link). still single rust binary. **WITHIN charter constraint** (dep surface essentially unchanged).

## risk
- sqlite-vec is young (~2024). API may shift. mitigation: pin version
- KNN performance at 3500 rows = trivial. at 100k+ rows = need ANN index (sqlite-vec has it)
- doesn't FORCE us to address category mismatches. evolve-don't-replace = also evolve-don't-rethink. is that a risk or feature? open Q.

# M2 open questions — unresolvable from paper alone

Surfaced during M2 paper-eval. None blocking the survey itself; ALL must be answered in M3 prototype before architecture decision.

## Q1: which embedding model
all six systems (where vec applies) require choice. options: BGE-M3 (multilingual, 1024d), nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (768d, MTEB strong), gte-large (1024d), OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536d, paid). Choice affects: precision (C3), index size, embedding latency, ops cost. Cannot select from paper. M3 must benchmark ≥2 on held-out query set.

## Q2: what is "held-out query set" for C3 (precision >50%)
viability criterion C3 demands measurement, but we have NO labeled retrieval-judgment set today. M3 prerequisite: construct ≥30 (query, expected_unit_ids) pairs from real lotus / simaris / agent sessions. Without this, C3 is unmeasurable for ANY candidate.

## Q3: full-primer token cost — measured vs estimated
M1 baseline measured aspect BODY size only. cya loads aspect + part_of children. true full-primer cost = body + sum(children bodies). 244 aspects × variable child counts = unmeasured. M3 prereq: write `simaris primer-size <aspect>` benchmark to populate the C4 column with real numbers across all 244 aspects.

## Q4: enforcement vs evolution — which orphans get adopted?
C2 (orphan <10%) requires either (a) DB constraint blocking new orphans + manual adoption pass for 2143 existing orphans, or (b) automated adoption (semantic clustering -> assign part_of). Path (a) requires ownership taxonomy decision (who owns what aspect tree). Path (b) requires similarity threshold tuning + human review. Storage choice does not pick the path — but Postgres / SQLite enable (a) cheaply, vec stores enable (b) cheaply.

## Q5: do we want LLM in the write path?
Mem0 forces this (extraction). pgvector / sqlite-vec / lance don't, but if we want auto-clustering for Q4 path (b), we'd add an embed call on every write. Cost + latency + offline-mode implications. Charter doesn't address. Need decision before M3.

## Q6: graph N-hop primer — current 1-hop sufficient?
all candidates can do N-hop via recursive CTE (SQL) or client loops (Lance/Qdrant). Question is whether 2-3 hop expansion improves retrieval enough to justify cost. M3 should include a 1-hop vs 2-hop precision delta on the held-out set.

## Q7: simaris-server REST API — port surface or rewrite?
M2 brief lists this surface but per-system surveys assumed it can layer over any backend. Real test: does `simaris-server`'s `/api/units/:id` semantics survive on each backend? For SQLite / Postgres: trivial. For Qdrant: payload model lossy. For Lance: fine. For Letta / Mem0: API model alien. Matrix scored implicitly; explicit prototype check needed in M3.

## Q8: backup/restore semantics across candidates
sqlite has VACUUM INTO + SQL dump (current path). pgvector has pg_dump. lance has versioned snapshots. qdrant has snapshot API. Letta / Mem0 unclear. Charter doesn't bind, but operational continuity matters. Decide in M3 candidate evaluation.

## Q9: what does "memory-architect cannot resolve from paper alone" mean for ranker noise?
M0 baseline noted auto-link tag-overlap noise (54 auto-links on a single add). All vec-capable candidates COULD replace tag-overlap auto-link with cosine-similarity rerank. Whether this fixes noise OR introduces different noise (semantic false positives) is testable, not predictable from docs.
